blocks.text: A full-time faculty may be allowed a maximum teaching load of 30 contact hours per week. However, a faculty
member with a documented Very Satisfactory teaching
performance may be allowed to handle additional six (6)
hours per week (CHED-MARINA Memorandum Circular No. 01, series of 2022).
Section 36 The computation oh honorarium based on the Prime
Hourly Teaching Rate (PHTR) for teaching overload in the
undergraduate program shall conform with Chapter 7,
Section 7.13 of the DBM Circular Letter No. 2007-6 (Manual on Position Compensation and Classification).
Section 37 The computation for overtime pay of the teaching overload
on a scheduled workday and on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall use the
formula as stipulated in the Joint Circular No. 02, s.2015 dated November 25, 2015 of the Civil Service Commission
and the Department of Budget and Management.
Section 38 Administrative personnel, regardless of employment

status, who wish to teach in the undergraduate program outside of the 8-hour work period shall be compensated on a fixed hourly rate based on the highest educational attainment and the expertise of the course(s) taught

Board Resolution No. 17-2005, Board pursuant to Resolution No. 44-205, Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016. The hourly rate as embodied in the aforementioned resolutions shall be subject to change should there be amendments in the succeeding year(s).
VII. Midyear Classes
Section 1 Faculty members assigned/requested to teach course(s)
in the undergraduate program during midyear break shall be allowed to handle a maximum of 2 courses/preparations or 9 teaching units.
Section 2 The compensation of honorarium based on the Prime
Hourly Teaching Rate (PHTR) for teaching overload in the
undergraduate program shall conform with Chapter 7,
Section 7,13 of the DBM Circular Letter No. 2007-6 (Manual on Position Compensation)

fixed_blocks.text:
19 A full-time faculty may be allowed a maximum teaching load of 30 contact hours per week. However, a faculty member with a documented Very Satisfactory teaching performance may be allowed to handle additional six (6) hours per week (CHED-MARINA Memorandum Circular No. 01, series of 2022).

Section 36 The computation oh honorarium based on the Prime Hourly Teaching Rate (PHTR) for teaching overload in the undergraduate program shall conform with Chapter 7, Section 7.13 of the DBM Circular Letter No. 2007-6 (Manual on Position Compensation and Classification).

Section 37 The computation for overtime pay of the teaching overload on a scheduled workday and on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall use the formula as stipulated in the Joint Circular No. 02, s.2015 dated November 25, 2015 of the Civil Service Commission and the Department of Budget and Management.

Section 38 Administrative personnel, regardless of employment status, who wish to teach in the undergraduate program outside of the 8-hour work period shall be compensated on a fixed hourly rate based on the highest educational attainment and the expertise of the course(s) taught Board Resolution No. 17-2005, Board pursuant to Resolution No. 44-205, Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016. The hourly rate as embodied in the aforementioned resolutions shall be subject to change should there be amendments in the succeeding year(s).

VII. Midyear Classes

Section 1 Faculty members assigned/requested to teach course(s) in the undergraduate program during midyear break shall be allowed to handle a maximum of 2 courses/preparations or 9 teaching units.

Section 2 The compensation of honorarium based on the Prime Hourly Teaching Rate (PHTR) for teaching overload in the undergraduate program shall conform with Chapter 7, Section 7.13 of the DBM Circular Letter No. 2007-6 (Manual on Position Compensation)

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum number of hours that a faculty member can handle beyond their maximum teaching load without prior approval from the university?
qa_pairs.answer: Beyond their maximum teaching load without prior approval from the university, a faculty member can handle up to 6 hours per week.

qa_pairs.question: What is the basis for computing the honorarium for administrative personnel who wish to teach in the undergraduate program on a midyear break?
qa_pairs.answer: The honorarium is computed based on the Prime Hourly Teaching Rate (PHTR) for teaching overload in the undergraduate program, as stipulated in Chapter 7, Section 7.13 of the DBM Circular Letter No. 2007-6.

qa_pairs.context:
19 A full-time faculty may be allowed a maximum teaching load of 30 contact hours per week. However, a faculty member with a documented Very Satisfactory teaching performance may be allowed to handle additional six (6) hours per week (CHED-MARINA Memorandum Circular No. 01, series of 2022).

Section 36 The computation oh honorarium based on the Prime Hourly Teaching Rate (PHTR) for teaching overload in the undergraduate program shall conform with Chapter 7, Section 7.13 of the DBM Circular Letter No. 2007-6 (Manual on Position Compensation and Classification).

Section 37 The computation for overtime pay of the teaching overload on a scheduled workday and on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall use the formula as stipulated in the Joint Circular No. 02, s.2015 dated November 25, 2015 of the Civil Service Commission and the Department of Budget and Management.

Section 38 Administrative personnel, regardless of employment status, who wish to teach in the undergraduate program outside of the 8-hour work period shall be compensated on a fixed hourly rate based on the highest educational attainment and the expertise of the course(s) taught Board Resolution No. 17-2005, Board pursuant to Resolution No. 44-205, Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016. The hourly rate as embodied in the aforementioned resolutions shall be subject to change should there be amendments in the succeeding year(s).

VII. Midyear Classes

Section 1 Faculty members assigned/requested to teach course(s) in the undergraduate program during midyear break shall be allowed to handle a maximum of 2 courses/preparations or 9 teaching units.

Section 2 The compensation of honorarium based on the Prime Hourly Teaching Rate (PHTR) for teaching overload in the undergraduate program shall conform with Chapter 7, Section 7.13 of the DBM Circular Letter No. 2007-6 (Manual on Position Compensation)
qa_pairs.question: What is the scope of the Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016?
qa_pairs.answer: Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016 determines the compensation for administrative personnel who wish to teach in the undergraduate program outside of the 8-hour work period.
